issues: Indentation errors in compute_signal method cause logic to be unreachable or incorrect.
compute_signal returns string signals ('HOLD', 'RISK_ON', 'RISK_OFF') instead of boolean or expected signal format per spec.
Signal logic triggers 'RISK_ON' only if pct_increase > 0.5, which matches spec trigger (>50%), but signal naming is inconsistent with spec intent.
Position exit logic correctly checks activity below 7-day MA or after 5 days, but uses 'RISK_OFF' signal instead of 'SELL' or similar as per spec intent.
target_allocations uses correct allocation per signal (5%) and max total exposure (20%), and allocates only to specified universe subset (ATVI, EA, NVDA).
Adapter 'fetch_reddit_activity' is correctly imported and used with allowed kwargs and output columns ('date', 'posts', 'comments').
Signal strings ('RISK_ON', 'RISK_OFF', 'HOLD') do not clearly match spec entry/exit naming ('Buy', 'Sell'), potentially causing integration issues.
